upload_failure.title: Wolta data upload failure
upload_failure.description: Wolta has been unable to upload energy data for more than 7 days. Last error: {error}. Check your internet connection and verify the integration is configured correctly.

profile_full.title: Wolta profile is full
profile_full.description: Your Wolta profile has reached its storage limit (about 2.3 years of 15-minute data), so new energy data can no longer be uploaded. Your grade and economy figures keep working on the stored history. To start fresh, remove the integration and set it up again.

measured_capacity.title: Wolta measured a different battery capacity

confirm.title: Adopt the measured battery capacity?
confirm.description:
Over {days} days, Wolta measured your battery's usable capacity at the meter as about {measured} kWh, but your configuration works out to about {configured} kWh.

The measured value is what your battery actually delivers to the house (AC side) — not the rated/nameplate or the cell figure your battery app may show at 100 %, which is higher because the inverter loses energy converting to and from the battery. The grade compares you against a perfect controller on your hardware, so this value should match what the battery really moves.

Adopt {measured} kWh? This sets the battery capacity and clears any reserve (the measured value already excludes it). If you simply haven't fully charged or discharged in this period, you can ignore this and dismiss the issue.

abort.entry_not_found: The Wolta integration this repair belonged to has been removed.

confirm.title: Set the battery's maximum power
confirm.description:
Over {days} days, the most power your battery has charged or discharged at the meter is about {measured} kW, but you configured {configured} kW.

This is only what the battery has been *seen* doing — if your control never demanded full power, the real maximum may be higher. Enter your battery's true maximum power (its inverter limit). A perfect controller would use all of it, so a value set too low would flatter your grade and one set too high would unfairly lower it. It's probably around {measured} kW — raise it if your battery can do more.

data_description.battery_kw: The battery's real maximum charge/discharge power at the meter (inverter limit). Pre-filled with the measured peak — adjust it if you know the battery can do more.

abort.entry_not_found: The Wolta integration this repair belonged to has been removed.

measured_efficiency.title: Wolta measured a different round-trip efficiency

confirm.title: Adopt the measured efficiency?
confirm.description:
Over {days} days, Wolta measured your battery's round-trip efficiency at the meter (energy out ÷ energy in) as about {measured}, but you configured {configured}.

The grade compares you against a perfect controller on your hardware, so the efficiency should match your battery's real AC round-trip. This can drift when it was set from thin history at setup. Adopt {measured}?

abort.entry_not_found: The Wolta integration this repair belonged to has been removed.

entities.title: Select energy entities
entities.description: Select the energy sensors that measure your battery, grid and solar production. Battery and grid sensors are required; solar is optional.

privacy.title: Data & privacy
privacy.description: Your 15-minute energy data is stored at Wolta until you remove the integration. It is never shared unless you tick the contribution checkbox below.

data.share: Contribute anonymised data to improve Wolta

reauth_confirm.title: Re-authenticate Wolta
reauth_confirm.description: Your Wolta profile token has expired or been deleted. Confirm to create a new profile and re-link this integration.

user.title: Set up Wolta
user.description: Connect Wolta for battery optimization grades and economy sensors.

menu_option_descriptions.create: Start from scratch – Home Assistant creates your Wolta profile.
menu_option_descriptions.link: Already used wolta.se? Paste your profile link or token and keep your existing history.

plant.title: Battery and economy
plant.description: Prefilled where possible from your Home Assistant location and sensor history – check the values and adjust.

data.cost_sek: Battery purchase price (kr) – battery only, optional
data.surcharge_ore: Electricity supplier markup (öre/ct per kWh) – optional

data_description.battery_kwh: Usable capacity **measured at your meter** — the kWh the battery actually delivers to the house on a full discharge (AC side). This is less than the nameplate rating, and less than the energy your battery app shows at 100 % SoC (that's the DC/cell figure), because the inverter loses energy converting to and from the battery. The grade compares you against a perfect controller on YOUR hardware, so use the meter-side usable value — not nameplate — to keep the comparison fair. Batteries with a reserve/SoC floor (e.g. Emaldo) deliver even less. With a few months of history, Wolta measures this for you and can suggest the value via a Repair.
data_description.nameplate_kwh: The manufacturer-rated capacity (e.g. sonnen 10p/22 = 22 kWh nameplate / 20 kWh usable). Used by the expansion calculator on wolta.se so per-kWh prices (quoted per nameplate kWh) are compared fairly against what the battery actually delivers. Leave empty if your battery is marketed by usable capacity (e.g. Tesla Powerwall).
data_description.nameplate_kw: The manufacturer-rated power (e.g. 8 kW). The model uses the deliverable AC power above; the rated figure lets wolta.se explain the difference when the measured power at the meter is lower (inverter losses, derating). Leave empty if your battery is marketed by deliverable power.
data_description.eff: Round-trip efficiency measured on the AC side (energy out ÷ energy in, at the meter). Not the DC/cell figure from the manufacturer app.
data_description.reserve_pct: The share of usable capacity your control system never discharges below (e.g. backup reserve). The grade benchmark then plays with the same window you do. Enter usable capacity above — the reserve is subtracted here, don't subtract it twice.
data_description.cost_sek: Purchase price of the BATTERY ONLY (not solar panels/installation) in SEK. Used to calculate the battery's IRR and payback. Optional. Bought the battery in stages, or want to record your solar cost and see the whole plant's return? Those are edited on your wolta.se profile.
data_description.purchase_date: Date the battery (or solar+battery) system was installed (YYYY-MM-DD). Used to calculate this year's actual savings. Optional. When suggested from your sensor history, the date is the start of the first recorded month — adjust if you know the exact date. Date in the future (a planned purchase)? The results only show the actual outcome so far — for a planned purchase, use the decision calculator on wolta.se instead.
data_description.grid_var_ore: Your variable grid fee, excluding VAT, in öre/kWh (SEK zones) or euro cents/kWh (other zones). Leave blank to use your country's standard tariff.
data_description.surcharge_ore: Your electricity supplier's markup, excluding VAT, in öre/kWh (SEK zones) or euro cents/kWh (other zones). Leave blank to use your country's standard tariff.
data_description.export_extra_ore: Extra amount your grid/export agreement pays (or deducts) per exported kWh, on top of the spot price, in öre or euro cents. Can be negative. Leave blank to use your country's standard tariff.
data_description.invert_battery: Turn on if your grade looks inverted (a strongly negative score). Swaps battery charge and discharge on upload, so you don't have to change your Home Assistant sensors.

link.title: Link your wolta.se profile
link.description: Paste your personal wolta.se link (the one with ?profile=…) or just the token. Home Assistant takes over data uploads from here – overlapping periods are overwritten with sensor data.

invert_check.title: Check battery sensor direction
invert_check.description: Your history shows more energy leaving the battery than entering it, which usually means the charge/discharge sensors are swapped (common with signed meters like Shelly or Emaldo). Keeping this on uploads the streams swapped so your grade is computed correctly.

reconfigure.title: Change energy sensors
reconfigure.description: Pick the new sensors. Home Assistant re-uploads your full history from the new sensors and your grade is recomputed – no need to remove the integration.

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to Wolta API – check your internet connection and try again.
error.invalid_input: Invalid battery configuration – battery capacity and power must both be greater than zero.
error.rate_limited: Wolta API is currently rate-limiting requests – please try again later.
error.required_sensor: At least one sensor is required for this stream.
error.invalid_token: Unknown or deleted profile token – check the link and try again.
error.profile_no_battery: This profile has no battery – the integration requires a battery profile. Solar-only profiles are not supported.
error.identity_conflict: This plant identity is already bound to another profile on wolta.se. Remove the other entry first, or link a different profile.

abort.reconfigure_successful: Sensors updated. Your history is being re-uploaded in the background.

init.title: Wolta profile
init.description: These values are your shared Wolta profile – changes here show up on wolta.se too, and changes made on wolta.se show up here. Clear an optional field to remove its value.

data_description.battery_kwh: Usable capacity **measured at your meter** — the kWh the battery actually delivers to the house on a full discharge (AC side). This is less than the nameplate rating, and less than the energy your battery app shows at 100 % SoC (that's the DC/cell figure), because the inverter loses energy converting to and from the battery. The grade compares you against a perfect controller on YOUR hardware, so use the meter-side usable value — not nameplate — to keep the comparison fair. Batteries with a reserve/SoC floor (e.g. Emaldo) deliver even less. With a few months of history, Wolta measures this for you and can suggest the value via a Repair.
data_description.nameplate_kwh: The manufacturer-rated capacity (e.g. sonnen 10p/22 = 22 kWh nameplate / 20 kWh usable). Used by the expansion calculator on wolta.se so per-kWh prices (quoted per nameplate kWh) are compared fairly against what the battery actually delivers. Leave empty if your battery is marketed by usable capacity (e.g. Tesla Powerwall).
data_description.nameplate_kw: The manufacturer-rated power (e.g. 8 kW). The model uses the deliverable AC power above; the rated figure lets wolta.se explain the difference when the measured power at the meter is lower (inverter losses, derating). Leave empty if your battery is marketed by deliverable power.
data_description.eff: Round-trip efficiency (0.5–1.0), measured on the AC side — not the DC/cell figure. Changing plant values triggers a server-side regrade.
data_description.reserve_pct: The share of usable capacity your control system never discharges below (e.g. backup reserve). The grade benchmark then plays with the same window you do. Enter usable capacity above — the reserve is subtracted here, don't subtract it twice. Clear the field to remove the reserve.

economy.name: Economy
economy.description: Entered a combined price for the whole plant (solar + battery) on wolta.se? Then the purchase price is managed there, and the price field is hidden here.

data_description.cost_sek: Purchase price of the BATTERY ONLY (not solar panels) in SEK. Used to calculate the battery's IRR and payback. Clear the field to remove the value.
data_description.purchase_date: Date the battery (or solar+battery) system was installed. Used to calculate this year's actual savings. Date in the future (a planned purchase)? The results only show the actual outcome so far — for a planned purchase, use the decision calculator on wolta.se instead. Clear the field to remove the value.

tariffs.name: Tariffs
tariffs.description: Leave blank to use your country's standard tariff.

data_description.grid_var_ore: Your variable grid fee, excluding VAT, in öre/kWh (SEK zones) or euro cents/kWh (other zones). Clear the field to revert to your country's standard tariff.
data_description.surcharge_ore: Your electricity supplier's markup, excluding VAT, in öre/kWh (SEK zones) or euro cents/kWh (other zones). Clear the field to revert to your country's standard tariff.
data_description.export_extra_ore: Extra amount your grid/export agreement pays (or deducts) per exported kWh, on top of the spot price, in öre or euro cents. Can be negative. Clear the field to revert to your country's standard tariff.

data_description.invert_battery: Turn on if your grade looks inverted (a strongly negative score). Swaps battery charge and discharge on upload, so you don't have to change your Home Assistant sensors.

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to Wolta API – check your internet connection and try again.

abort.cannot_connect: Could not reach wolta.se – profile editing needs a connection. Try again later.
abort.reauth_required: The profile no longer exists on wolta.se – re-authentication has been started, check your notifications.

recompute_failed.message: Wolta could not recompute right now – try again later
